:::{#thm-logit-expit}
#### logit and expit are each others' inverses

$$\logitf{\expitf{\logodds}} = \logodds$$

$$\expitf{\logitf{\pi}} = \pi$$

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{.proof}
First, we show that $\logitf{\expitf{\logodds}} = \logodds$,
using @def-logit-fn, @def-expit, and @cor-invodds-odds-inv:

$$
\ba
\logitf{\expitf{\logodds}}
&\eqdef \logf{\oddsf{\expitf{\logodds}}}
&& \text{(definition of logit, applied to } \prob = \expitf{\logodds}\text{)}
\\ &= \logf{\oddsf{\invoddsf{\expf{\logodds}}}}
&& \text{(definition of expit)}
\\ &= \logf{\expf{\logodds}}
&& \text{(the odds function inverts the inverse-odds function)}
\\ &= \logodds
&& \text{(the natural logarithm inverts the exponential function)}
\ea
$$

Next, we show that $\expitf{\logitf{\prob}} = \prob$,
using @def-expit, @def-logit-fn, and @cor-invodds-odds-inv:

$$
\ba
\expitf{\logitf{\prob}}
&\eqdef \invoddsf{\expf{\logitf{\prob}}}
&& \text{(definition of expit, applied to } \logodds = \logitf{\prob}\text{)}
\\ &= \invoddsf{\expf{\logf{\oddsf{\prob}}}}
&& \text{(definition of logit)}
\\ &= \invoddsf{\oddsf{\prob}}
&& \text{(the exponential function inverts the natural logarithm)}
\\ &= \prob
&& \text{(the inverse-odds function inverts the odds function)}
\ea
$$
:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#exm-logit-expit-numeric}

#### Numeric confirmation of the inverse relationship

Combining @exm-logit-numeric and @exm-expit-numeric
confirms @thm-logit-expit numerically:
$\expitf{\logitf{0.75}} = \expitf{\logf{3}} = 0.75$.

:::
